immediate_rejection_findings.title: PRIVILEGE ESCALATION WITHOUT VALIDATION
immediate_rejection_findings.flaw: Binary requires root for SCHED_FIFO and mlockall but performs no privilege dropping after initialization. No capability bounding set, no seccomp filtering.
immediate_rejection_findings.risk: Full root compromise if any vulnerability is exploited in the application. Violates principle of least privilege.
immediate_rejection_findings.why_rejection_grade: Security-critical systems must minimize attack surface. Running as root indefinitely is unacceptable for any deployment scenario.
immediate_rejection_findings.remediation: Implement privilege dropping after initialization using cap_setuid/cap_setgid. Drop to unprivileged user. Use seccomp-bpf to restrict syscalls. Set capability bounding set to minimum required.

immediate_rejection_findings.title: MEMORY LOCK WITHOUT BOUNDS
immediate_rejection_findings.flaw: mlockall(MCL_CURRENT | MCL_FUTURE) with RLIM_INFINITY — no memory limits enforced.
immediate_rejection_findings.risk: Memory exhaustion attacks; system instability; denial of service to other processes.
immediate_rejection_findings.why_rejection_grade: No upper bound on locked memory could lock entire system RAM, causing system-wide denial of service.
immediate_rejection_findings.remediation: Set explicit RLIMIT_MEMLOCK to reasonable bound (e.g., 64MB). Validate against available RAM. Implement memory pressure monitoring.

immediate_rejection_findings.title: UNSAFE MPMC IMPLEMENTATION
immediate_rejection_findings.flaw: MPMCRingBuffer uses std::mutex but elements are not atomic — race condition on element access. Mutex protects head/tail but not element copy operation.
immediate_rejection_findings.risk: Data corruption, torn reads/writes in multi-producer scenarios. Non-atomic read of potentially multi-word types.
immediate_rejection_findings.why_rejection_grade: Lock-free data structures must guarantee atomicity. This implementation provides false security.
immediate_rejection_findings.remediation: Use std::atomic<T> for elements if T is trivially copyable and fits in atomic word. Otherwise, extend mutex coverage to include element access. Consider using boost::lockfree::spsc_queue or similar proven implementation.

immediate_rejection_findings.title: TIMESTAMP WRAP VULNERABILITY
immediate_rejection_findings.flaw: TimestampNs is uint64_t with no overflow checking. Intermediate calculations (tv_sec * 1000000000UL + tv_nsec) can overflow before assignment.
immediate_rejection_findings.risk: Timing calculations overflow; deadline monitoring fails silently; potential negative latency values.
immediate_rejection_findings.why_rejection_grade: Safety systems must handle time correctly. Silent overflow is unacceptable.
immediate_rejection_findings.remediation: Add overflow checking using __builtin_mul_overflow or equivalent. Use std::chrono::nanoseconds for type-safe time arithmetic. Add runtime assertions for timestamp monotonicity.

immediate_rejection_findings.title: RACE CONDITION IN SAFETY MONITOR
immediate_rejection_findings.flaw: update_vision_frame() and update_actuation_frame() update multiple atomics non-atomically. run_cycle() reads them independently. Sequence number and timestamp are separate atomics with no memory barrier between updates.
immediate_rejection_findings.risk: Safety monitor may observe inconsistent state (new sequence with old timestamp or vice versa). False negatives on fault detection.
immediate_rejection_findings.why_rejection_grade: Safety monitor is the last line of defense. Race conditions in safety code are unacceptable.
immediate_rejection_findings.remediation: Use std::atomic<FrameState> where FrameState contains both sequence and timestamp. Or use mutex to protect the update-read critical section. Add memory barriers explicitly.

immediate_rejection_findings.title: ZERO-COPY DMA WITHOUT VALIDATION
immediate_rejection_findings.flaw: ZeroCopyFrame contains raw pointers to DMA memory with no bounds validation. No validation that plane_data[i] + plane_size[i] stays within allocated DMA buffer.
immediate_rejection_findings.risk: Buffer overread if plane_size is corrupted. Arbitrary memory disclosure. Potential for DMA buffer corruption.
immediate_rejection_findings.why_rejection_grade: Zero-copy is an optimization, not a safety feature. Unvalidated pointers violate memory safety.
immediate_rejection_findings.remediation: Add buffer descriptor with base address and size. Validate all pointer arithmetic against bounds. Use span<T> or gsl::span for bounds-checked access.

immediate_rejection_findings.title: I2C COMMUNICATION WITHOUT AUTHENTICATION
immediate_rejection_findings.flaw: Direct I2C writes to gimbal controller with no message authentication. No HMAC, no sequence numbers, no replay protection on safety-critical actuator commands.
immediate_rejection_findings.risk: Spoofed gimbal commands via I2C bus injection. Unauthorized actuation. Replay attacks causing unintended movement.
immediate_rejection_findings.why_rejection_grade: Actuator commands are safety-critical. Unauthenticated commands violate basic security principles.
immediate_rejection_findings.remediation: Implement message authentication (HMAC-SHA256) for all actuator commands. Add sequence numbers for replay protection. Consider hardware interlock that requires cryptographic challenge-response.

immediate_rejection_findings.title: UNIX SOCKET WITHOUT ACCESS CONTROL
immediate_rejection_findings.flaw: Socket at /tmp/aurore_hud.sock with default permissions. No client authentication. No chmod on socket. No credential checking via SO_PEERCRED. No encryption.
immediate_rejection_findings.risk: Information disclosure (telemetry data). Potential injection of fake HUD data. Unauthorized access to system status.
immediate_rejection_findings.why_rejection_grade: Inter-process communication must be authenticated. Default socket permissions are insecure.
immediate_rejection_findings.remediation: Set socket permissions to 0600. Use SO_PEERCRED to verify client UID/GID. Implement application-level authentication. Consider using abstract namespace sockets for additional isolation.

immediate_rejection_findings.title: FIXED-SIZE BUFFER OVERFLOW RISK
immediate_rejection_findings.flaw: CsvLogEntry::copy_to_array uses strncpy but module/event fields are fixed 32 bytes. strncpy doesn't guarantee null termination when source >= dest size.
immediate_rejection_findings.risk: Truncation without null terminator if source is exactly 32 bytes. Buffer overread when string is later used. Potential information disclosure.
immediate_rejection_findings.why_rejection_grade: String handling bugs are a leading cause of vulnerabilities. This is a known strncpy pitfall.
immediate_rejection_findings.remediation: Use snprintf or strlcpy (if available). Always explicitly null-terminate: dest[max_len-1] = '\0'. Consider using std::string or std::string_view for safer string handling.

immediate_rejection_findings.title: ASYNC QUEUE WITHOUT BACKPRESSURE HANDLING
immediate_rejection_findings.flaw: Queue drops oldest entries when full but no alerting mechanism. entries_dropped_ counter exists but no callback, no fault trigger, no alert.
immediate_rejection_findings.risk: Silent data loss. Audit trail gaps. Forensics impossible after incident. Compliance violations.
immediate_rejection_findings.why_rejection_grade: Audit logging is a security control. Silent failure of security controls is unacceptable.
immediate_rejection_findings.remediation: Implement backpressure that blocks producer when queue is full. Or implement fault trigger when drop threshold exceeded. Log drop events to separate non-volatile storage.

high_risk_latent_failures.title: THREAD JOIN WITH TIMEOUT — RESOURCE LEAK
high_risk_latent_failures.latent_defect: join_with_timeout detaches threads that don't terminate. Detached threads may access freed resources after main exits.
high_risk_latent_failures.trigger_condition: Shutdown during active camera/telemetry operations. Thread deadlock or long-blocking I/O.
high_risk_latent_failures.consequence: Use-after-free. Crash during shutdown. Corrupted telemetry files. Undefined behavior.
high_risk_latent_failures.mitigation: Use std::jthread (C++20) for automatic joining. Implement proper shutdown signaling with timeouts. Never detach threads that access shared resources.

high_risk_latent_failures.title: WATCHDOG WITHOUT HARDWARE BACKUP
high_risk_latent_failures.latent_defect: Software watchdog runs on same CPU as monitored threads. Single point of failure.
high_risk_latent_failures.trigger_condition: CPU core hang. Kernel panic. Thermal throttling. Memory corruption affecting safety monitor.
high_risk_latent_failures.consequence: Safety monitor fails silently. No emergency stop triggered. System continues in unsafe state.
high_risk_latent_failures.mitigation: Implement external hardware watchdog (GPIO toggle). Use separate microcontroller for safety monitoring. Implement dual-core lockstep comparison.

high_risk_latent_failures.title: LOCK-FREE BUFFER — ABA PROBLEM
high_risk_latent_failures.latent_defect: SPSC ring buffer vulnerable to ABA problem if producer wraps multiple times before consumer reads.
high_risk_latent_failures.trigger_condition: Consumer thread stalled > 4 frames (buffer capacity). Producer continues at 120Hz.
high_risk_latent_failures.consequence: Silent data corruption. Stale frame processed as new. Incorrect tracking data.
high_risk_latent_failures.mitigation: Use tagged pointers with version counter. Or use larger buffer to reduce wrap probability. Implement sequence number validation in consumer.

high_risk_latent_failures.title: STUB IMPLEMENTATION IN PRODUCTION PATH
high_risk_latent_failures.latent_defect: capture_frame_stub returns valid=false but callers may not check. wrap_as_mat returns empty Mat for invalid frames.
high_risk_latent_failures.trigger_condition: Hardware unavailable. Fallback to stub. Caller doesn't check return value.
high_risk_latent_failures.consequence: Invalid frames processed. Garbage telemetry. Potential null pointer dereference in downstream code.
high_risk_latent_failures.mitigation: Make valid check mandatory with [[nodiscard]]. Add runtime assertions. Fail fast on invalid frames rather than propagating garbage.

high_risk_latent_failures.title: MONTE CARLO WITH FIXED SEED
high_risk_latent_failures.latent_defect: std::mt19937 rng(42) — deterministic 'random' numbers. p_hit values are not true statistical analysis.
high_risk_latent_failures.trigger_condition: Any use of monte_carlo_p_hit. Same inputs always produce same outputs.
high_risk_latent_failures.consequence: p_hit values are predictable. Misleading safety metrics. False confidence in solution quality.
high_risk_latent_failures.mitigation: Use std::random_device for seeding. Or use compile-time random seed. Document that p_hit is for debugging, not safety certification.

high_risk_latent_failures.title: CLIENT FD LEAK ON DISCONNECT
high_risk_latent_failures.latent_defect: Dead client detection only happens on send. Clients that disconnect silently never removed from client_fds_.
high_risk_latent_failures.trigger_condition: Client crashes without proper socket close. Network partition.
high_risk_latent_failures.consequence: FD exhaustion. Denial of service. Memory leak. send() eventually fails for all clients.
high_risk_latent_failures.mitigation: Use SO_KEEPALIVE socket option. Implement periodic heartbeat. Use epoll/poll for disconnect detection. Close and remove clients on any error.

high_risk_latent_failures.title: CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW ASSUMPTION
high_risk_latent_failures.latent_defect: Assumes CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW is available. No fallback for systems without it. get_timestamp throws exception on failure.
high_risk_latent_failures.trigger_condition: Deployment on kernel without CLOCK_MONOTONIC_RAW support. Container with restricted clock access.
high_risk_latent_failures.consequence: get_timestamp throws exception. Application crash. No graceful degradation.
high_risk_latent_failures.mitigation: Add runtime detection of clock availability. Fallback to CLOCK_MONOTONIC if RAW unavailable. Return error code instead of throwing in real-time paths.

high_risk_latent_failures.title: STATE TRANSITION WITHOUT ATOMICITY
high_risk_latent_failures.latent_defect: transition() updates state_ then calls enter_state() — not atomic. Concurrent tick() and event handler calls can observe inconsistent state.
high_risk_latent_failures.trigger_condition: Multi-threaded access to state machine. Event arrives during state transition.
high_risk_latent_failures.consequence: Race condition. Inconsistent state observation. Incorrect behavior based on stale state.
high_risk_latent_failures.mitigation: Protect state machine with mutex. Or use atomic state with version counter. Ensure all access is from single thread (event loop pattern).

required_corrective_actions.required_action: Must implement privilege dropping after initialization. Drop root privileges using cap_setuid/cap_setgid. Set capability bounding set to minimum required (CAP_SYS_NICE, CAP_NET_RAW). Implement seccomp-bpf filtering.
required_corrective_actions.acceptance_criterion: Binary runs as unprivileged user after init. strace shows no syscalls outside seccomp whitelist. capsh --print shows minimal capabilities.

required_corrective_actions.required_action: Must set explicit RLIMIT_MEMLOCK to reasonable bound (e.g., 64MB). Validate against available RAM at startup. Implement memory pressure monitoring with graceful degradation.
required_corrective_actions.acceptance_criterion: getrlimit(RLIMIT_MEMLOCK) returns bounded value. Application fails gracefully if memory unavailable. /proc/meminfo shows locked memory within bounds.

required_corrective_actions.required_action: Must fix MPMC implementation to protect element access. Extend mutex coverage or use atomic elements. Document thread-safety guarantees.
required_corrective_actions.acceptance_criterion: ThreadSanitizer reports no data races with multiple producers/consumers. Element integrity verified under stress test.

required_corrective_actions.required_action: Must add overflow checking for timestamp calculations. Use std::chrono::nanoseconds for type-safe arithmetic. Add runtime assertions for timestamp monotonicity.
required_corrective_actions.acceptance_criterion: Overflow detected and handled gracefully. Monotonicity violations logged and trigger fault. No undefined behavior under TSAN.

required_corrective_actions.required_action: Must make frame updates atomic. Use std::atomic<FrameState> or mutex protection. Add memory barriers explicitly.
required_corrective_actions.acceptance_criterion: ThreadSanitizer reports no races. Safety monitor observes consistent state under stress. Fault detection verified with injected faults.

required_corrective_actions.required_action: Must add DMA buffer bounds validation. Use span<T> or gsl::span for bounds-checked access. Validate all pointer arithmetic.
required_corrective_actions.acceptance_criterion: AddressSanitizer reports no out-of-bounds access. Bounds violations trigger fault. Zero-copy invariant maintained.

required_corrective_actions.required_action: Must implement message authentication for I2C commands. Add HMAC-SHA256. Add sequence numbers for replay protection.
required_corrective_actions.acceptance_criterion: Spoofed commands rejected. Replay attacks detected. Performance impact within timing budget.

required_corrective_actions.required_action: Must set socket permissions to 0600. Use SO_PEERCRED for client authentication. Implement application-level auth.
required_corrective_actions.acceptance_criterion: Unauthorized clients rejected. ls -l shows 0600 permissions. SO_PEERCRED verification logged.

required_corrective_actions.required_action: Must fix string copy to always null-terminate. Use snprintf or strlcpy. Add explicit null termination.
required_corrective_actions.acceptance_criterion: AddressSanitizer reports no overread. Long strings handled correctly. No truncation bugs.

required_corrective_actions.required_action: Must implement backpressure or fault triggering on queue overflow. Log drop events to non-volatile storage.
required_corrective_actions.acceptance_criterion: No silent data loss. Drop events logged. Producer blocks or fault triggered under sustained overload.

medium_risk_findings.title: SECURITY FLAGS INCOMPLETE
medium_risk_findings.finding: -D_FORTIFY_SOURCE=2 but no -U_FORTIFY_SOURCE for Release builds; may conflict with optimization.
medium_risk_findings.recommendation: Add explicit fortify level control per build type.

medium_risk_findings.title: HARDCODED PATHS
medium_risk_findings.finding: Paths like /var/log/aurore/, /run/aurore/ hardcoded; no validation at runtime.
medium_risk_findings.recommendation: Validate paths are writable; use XDG base directories.

medium_risk_findings.title: SCRIPT INJECTION VECTORS
medium_risk_findings.finding: Shell scripts use unquoted variables in some places; potential word-splitting.
medium_risk_findings.recommendation: Quote all variable expansions; use set -euo pipefail.

medium_risk_findings.title: MAGIC NUMBERS WITHOUT CONSTANTS
medium_risk_findings.finding: Values like 0.7f, 0.85f, 0.95f used directly in comparisons.

medium_risk_findings.title: UNBOUNDED LOOP IN RANSAC
medium_risk_findings.finding: cv::findHomography with RANSAC — OpenCV internal iteration count not bounded.
medium_risk_findings.recommendation: Set maxIters parameter explicitly; document WCET impact.

low_risk_findings.title: ENUM WITHOUT BOUNDS CHECK
low_risk_findings.finding: TelemetryEventId and TelemetrySeverity used without validation.

low_risk_findings.title: STATUS OUTPUT TO STDOUT
low_risk_findings.finding: std::cout used for status; may block if terminal unavailable.
low_risk_findings.recommendation: Use async logging; never block real-time threads on I/O.

low_risk_findings.title: NO I2C ERROR RECOVERY
low_risk_findings.finding: write_pwm_channel returns bool but callers don't check.
low_risk_findings.recommendation: Add retry logic; log failures to telemetry.

compliance_gaps.requirement_text: All inter-component messages shall be authenticated using HMAC-SHA256 with 256-bit keys.
compliance_gaps.gap: No HMAC on any interface. All I2C, socket, and shared memory communication is unauthenticated.

compliance_gaps.requirement_text: System shall implement replay attack prevention using monotonically increasing sequence numbers on all authenticated messages.
compliance_gaps.gap: No sequence numbers implemented anywhere. Replay attacks possible on all interfaces.

compliance_gaps.requirement_text: Audit log entry format shall be: {timestamp: u64, event_id: u16, severity: u8, data: u8[], hmac: u32[8]}. Each entry shall be individually signed.
compliance_gaps.gap: Telemetry exists but no HMAC per entry. Log integrity not protected.

compliance_gaps.requirement_text: Keys shall be generated using hardware RNG (or /dev/urandom) with ≥ 256 bits entropy. Keys shall never be stored in plaintext.
compliance_gaps.gap: No cryptographic keys implemented. No key management infrastructure.

compliance_gaps.requirement_text: System timing shall be bounded with: (a) WCET ≤ 5.0ms per AM7-L2-TIM-002, (b) jitter ≤ 5% at 99.9th percentile.
compliance_gaps.gap: WCET analysis script exists but no verified bounds. No statistical WCET analysis completed.

clearance_rationale: CRITICAL: 10 immediate rejection findings identified. All are security-critical vulnerabilities that must be remediated before any deployment. Key issues include: (1) privilege escalation without dropping, (2) unauthenticated actuator commands, (3) race conditions in safety monitor, (4) unauthenticated IPC, (5) memory safety violations. Additionally, 8 high-risk latent failures could cause field failures. Compliance gaps show 4/5 security requirements completely unimplemented.
stop_justification: This artifact must NOT proceed to implementation or deployment. The codebase contains multiple critical security vulnerabilities that would allow: (1) full system compromise via privilege escalation, (2) unauthorized actuator control via I2C injection, (3) safety system bypass via race conditions, (4) information disclosure via unauthenticated sockets. The safety monitor — the last line of defense — has race conditions that could prevent emergency stop activation. Remediation of all 10 critical findings is required before any hardware testing. Remediation of all 8 high-risk findings is required before any field deployment.
